generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing point clouds for obstacle detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor status and encoder readings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusTransmitted
]

instance SteeringSignalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing steering wheel angle and actuator status"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringSignalTransmitted
]

instance ServoSignalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring servo motor signals"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoSignalTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor providing vehicle state telemetry (speed, GPS, IMU)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemTransmitted
]

// Physical-to-virtual Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance MotorStatusTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringSignalTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSignalSensor
]

instance ServoSignalTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoSignalSensor
]

instance VehicleStateTelemTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateTelemetrySensor
]

// Data after translation and unit conversion
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud data serialized and unit‑converted by the Data Translation component for digital twin consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor status data serialized and unit‑converted for digital twin consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SteeringSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering signal data serialized and unit‑converted for digital twin consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringSignalTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ServoSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Servo signal data serialized and unit‑converted for digital twin consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoSignalTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateTelemData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state telemetry data serialized and unit‑converted for digital twin consumption"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// Enabler: Data Translation and Quality Assurance
instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component that serializes each stream, aligns message types to ISO 23247 Data Translation FE format, performs unit conversions, and ensures data quality"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

// Digital Twin Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface displaying real‑time vehicle state, projected trajectory, and object tracks; allows viewing, saving, and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ViewVehicleStateAction, GenerateReportAction, SaveTelemetryAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC algorithm to digital twin; computes lead‑vehicle detection and desired speed; returns speed commands to vehicle's ECU"
    DTDFVocab:provides OffloadACCAlgorithmAction, ReturnSpeedCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote management enabling operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking via low‑latency channel"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, IssueEmergencyBrakeAction
]

// Actions and Insights
instance ViewVehicleStateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "View real‑time vehicle state, projected trajectory, and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generate a report of the vehicle's telemetry data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SaveTelemetryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Save telemetry data for offline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OffloadACCAlgorithmAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offload ACC algorithm to digital twin; compute lead‑vehicle detection and desired speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReturnSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Return computed speed command to vehicle's ECU"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable Adaptive Cruise Control remotely"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Disable Adaptive Cruise Control remotely"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set desired speed remotely via the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issue emergency braking command via digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Digital Twin computation and data exchange are synchronized using a shared time reference that must be accurate within 100 ms, as mandated by the V2X safety and computation offloading requirements. All data packages exchanged between the Physical Twin Entity and the Digital Twin Entity via ROS2 are timestamped in this reference frame, guaranteeing that time‑stamped sensor samples, point‑cloud clustering results, and control commands remain coherent across devices. This 100 ms clock accuracy constraint defines the temporal granularity of the DT services; higher‑frequency updates (e.g., 1 ms for automotive safety loops) are out of scope due to hardware limitations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT is orchestrated following ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping functional entities (FEs) to individual ROS2 packages containing one or more ROS2 nodes. The User Entity hosts the User Interface FE subdivided into a 3D visualization FE and a control‑panel FE; the Digital Twin Entity provides data‑management, pre‑processing, and control logic; and the Physical Twin Entity implements low‑level automation such as point‑cloud clustering/tracking and device communication. Each FE is packaged as a ROS2 node, and cross‑entity interaction is realized through ROS2 topics/services, providing both message‑based publish/subscribe and request/response patterns to coordinate computation and state."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin Services use a ROS 2‑based architecture; internal component communication takes place over ROS 2 topics leveraging DDS for message exchange, while external communication between the digital and physical twin entities occurs over Wi‑Fi using UDP as the transport protocol. The Data Translation component serializes control and sensor messages, aligning them across both layers. Although the documents do not specify exact latency or bandwidth requirements, real‑time dashboards imply sub‑100 ms end‑to‑end delay, and the LiDAR point‑cloud rate typically requires 10–30 Mbps. UDP provides best‑effort delivery; the system lacks a described reliability or flow‑control mechanism, and security protocols are not addressed in the provided materials."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "This Digital Twin system implements the ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for manufacturing standard. It adopts the reference architecture defined in Part 2 of the ISO 23247 standard, organizing the Twin into an entity‑based structure comprising the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Each entity is further decomposed into Functional Entities responsible for tasks such as OME actuation, data transformation, and user interaction. In terms of communication and interoperability, the system supports OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) as a viable implementation for data exchange and device control, following the specifications of the OPC UA standard. The architecture aligns with interoperability standards required for manufacturing digital twins, ensuring compatibility with industrial equipment and data sources. No additional certification or compliance frameworks are specified in the provided documents."
]
